title: Machaerina affinis
taxonomy: Machaerina affinis is classified as a species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is further classified under the genus Machaerina, which is a group of sedge plants. The Machaerina affinis is an aquatic plant native to Australia and New Guinea. It is also known to grow in other regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. This species is recognized as a type of wetland plant, playing an essential role in its ecosystem.
